accurate.why: All claims align with the bill text and accurately reference the FDA and named statutes.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; context such as FDA administration is accurate.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the bill ensures overdose reversal agent references aren't limited to naloxone.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Names the 21st Century Cures Act and Public Health Service Act programs affected.

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes the FDA-approval condition and that updates apply specifically to two named grant programs.

effective_dates.why: Correctly states the one-year deadline for updating existing references.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures both the prospective requirement and the retrospective update obligation.

coherent.why: Well-organized and internally consistent despite a minor typo ('thatreferences').

concise.why: Compact with no padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: HHS, FDA, and the named statutes are all correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: The one-year deadline and section 505 reference are stated correctly.
